methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Ukrspecsystems is a Growth-stage defense and security company founded by Yuri Pashchenko and capitalized through Ministry of Defense contracts. The valuation exercise draws from a Defense & Security sector universe of 21 peers, including 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms such as Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

The peer-median approach was not applicable to this valuation. The narrow filter for same-stage and adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security yielded insufficient datapoints—zero same-stage matches, one adjacent-stage match, and zero similar-valuation-tier matches—falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for statistical credibility. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable heterogeneity bias across stage and business model. Consequently, no triangulated valuation range can be derived from the available comparable data.

This analysis is constrained by the absence of sufficient stage-matched peers. A credible valuation would require either: additional private market transactions at comparable stage and scale within Defense & Security; direct measurement of revenue or EBITDA multiples from the existing public peer set calibrated to Ukrspecsystems' growth profile; or income-based methods anchored to contract backlog and MoD procurement visibility. Without these data elements, quantitative valuation synthesis is not supportable.
